heading: Contract to make payments
label: 2394 Contract to make payments
text: The Administrator is authorized, without regard to sections 1341, 1342, and 1349–1351 and subchapter II of chapter 15 of title 31, to enter into a contract with any governmental or other entity to which payments are authorized to be made pursuant to , obligating the Administrator to make to such entity the payments directed or authorized to be made by : , That the term of such contracts, in the case of the cities of Oak Ridge, Tennessee, and Richland, Washington, and the Richland School District, shall not extend beyond ; and in the case of the Los Alamos School Board shall not extend beyond ; and in the case of the county of Los Alamos, New Mexico, shall not extend beyond . The authority to enter into a contract under the preceding sentence with the Los Alamos School Board and with the county of Los Alamos, New Mexico, shall be effective with respect to a period before , only to the extent or in such amounts as are provided in appropriation Acts.section 2391 of this titlesection 2391 of this titleProvided, howeverJune 30, 1979June 30, 1997June 30, 1997July 1, 1997
notes: Codification“Sections 1341, 1342, and 1349–1351 and subchapter II of chapter 15 of title 31” substituted in text for “section 3679 of the Revised Statutes []” on authority of , , , the first section of which enacted Title 31, Money and Finance.31 U.S.C. 665Pub. L. 97–258, § 4(b)96 Stat. 1067Sept. 13, 1982

Amendments1996— substituted “” for “” in two places and “” for “”.Pub. L. 104–106June 30, 1997June 30, 1996July 1, 1997July 1, 19961986—, substituted “Los Alamos School Board shall not extend beyond ” for “Los Alamos School Board shall not extend beyond ” and “New Mexico, shall not extend beyond ” for “New Mexico, shall not extend beyond ”.Pub. L. 99–661, § 3138(a)June 30, 1996June 30, 1986June 30, 1996June 30, 1987, inserted at end “The authority to enter into a contract under the preceding sentence with the Los Alamos School Board and with the county of Los Alamos, New Mexico, shall be effective with respect to a period before , only to the extent or in such amounts as are provided in appropriation Acts.”Pub. L. 99–661, § 3138(b)(1)July 1, 19961978— inserted provisions for applicability to the Los Alamos School Board and the county of Los Alamos, New Mexico, substituted “payments are authorized” for “payments are required or authorized”, and struck out applicability of provisions to Anderson and Roane Counties, Tennessee, for contracts not beyond .Pub. L. 95–238June 30, 19861975— substituted “Administrator” for “Commission” in two places and inserted provision that the term of such contracts shall not extend beyond , in the case of Anderson County and Roane County, Tennessee. The latter amendment was executed in this section, rather than to section 2393, as the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 94–187June 30, 19861967— inserted proviso, and “or authorized” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 90–190

Effective Date of 1986 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–661, div. C, title I, § 3138(b)(2)100 Stat. 4066Nov. 14, 1986“The amendment made by paragraph (1) [amending this section] shall not apply with respect to a contract with the county of Los Alamos, New Mexico, to the extent that it covers the period before .”July 1, 1987

Transfer of FunctionsEnergy Research and Development Administration terminated and functions vested by law in Administrator thereof transferred to Secretary of Energy (unless otherwise specifically provided) by sections 7151(a) and 7293 of this title.
